definition: Communication directions between the network and user equipment. Uplink is data sent from the UE to the network; downlink is data travelling from the network to UEs.

The control plane handles planning and instructions but does not carry actual user data. It contains commands for how the user and their data should be handled, receives real-time scheduling instructions (beamforming weights, PRB allocations) from the DU, and tells the RU what UE data to modulate or demodulate.

The user plane is the pipeline where actual user data flows as IQ samples. On downlink it unpacks DU IQ samples for RF transmission; on uplink it converts received waveforms into IQ vectors sent to the DU over fronthaul. It often relies on high-throughput hardware such as smartNIC FPGA or DPDK.

Radio Unit — the physical antenna that talks directly with UEs. It converts analog RF to digital IQ samples and forwards them to the DU over ethernet, and modulates/demodulates U-Plane data per DU instructions.
The connection between the RU and DU carrying low-level radio data, often IQ samples over protocols like eCPRI or Open Fronthaul.
Enhanced Common Public Radio Interface — a protocol that packages IQ samples into standard ethernet frames for efficient transport on fiber between RU and DU.
A schema language for telecom messages: a strict protocol blueprint defining what messages exist, their fields, mandatory vs optional fields, choices, and value types.
The message body wrapped inside UL-DCCH-Message → message → c1.
Uplink (UE → eNB) dedicated control channel message — an LTE RRC ASN.1 container for UE-to-eNB dedicated RRC messages sent after a dedicated RRC connection exists.
Uplink common control channel message used before a dedicated RRC connection exists, e.g. rrcConnectionRequest.
Python ASN.1 toolkit used to load UL-DCCH-Message definitions and validate that reconstructed messages match the expected ASN.1 structure and fields.
Public Land Mobile Network — identifies a mobile operator network via MCC (mobile country code) and MNC (mobile network code). Example: 302/221 is TELUS in Canada.
S1 Application Protocol — a control-plane protocol between 4G eNB and EPC/MME handling communications between the base station and core network.
srsRAN's simplified 4G core containing MME, SGW, PGW, and HSS. Authenticates srsUE and assigns an IP address.
Simulated radio link over network sockets where radio signals are represented as IQ samples with in-phase (I) and quadrature (Q) components.
A fake RF driver that pipes IQ samples over ZeroMQ sockets instead of real SDR hardware, enabling a digital twin on a plain server.
Software-defined radio — programmable radio hardware controlled by software, used to transmit/receive real RF signals instead of ZMQ simulation.
International Mobile Subscriber Identity — uniquely identifies a SIM/subscriber attempting to attach.
Secret authentication key stored in the SIM and operator subscriber database.
Operator-specific authentication value used with Milenage to prove the UE/SIM and network share the correct secret.
Subscriber identity and secrets needed for network authentication: IMSI, Ki, OP/OPc, and the authentication algorithm.
Home Subscriber Server — the 4G subscriber database storing IMSI, Ki, OPc, allowed APN, and QoS. The MME queries HSS during attach for validity and authentication challenges.
Unified Data Management — the 5G equivalent of the HSS subscriber database.
Adding subscriber records (IMSI, keys, profiles) into the network subscriber database.
3rd Generation Partnership Project — the international standards body defining mobile network protocols. srsRAN and OCUDU implement 3GPP specifications.
URLs on your server that another system calls automatically when an event occurs (push), instead of the client polling repeatedly.
One carrier on one sector of a base station — a single radio coverage unit defined by frequency and physical cell ID. Multi-UE on one cell means many UEs contending on that single virtual cell.
TX = transmit, RX = receive. A stream is the continuous flow of IQ samples. UE TX (uplink) must reach gNB RX; gNB TX (downlink) must reach each UE RX.
Signal-processing pipeline that sums many UE uplink TX streams into one gNB RX input and fans gNB downlink TX out to every UE RX — replicating over-the-air combining in software.

Distributed Unit — runs lower real-time layers (RLC, MAC, high-PHY scheduling) in an O-RAN split. A software DU runs on general-purpose servers; non-real-time simulation DUs can be time-dilated, real-time DUs driving real fronthaul cannot.
Centralized Unit — higher layers (PDCP/RRC) in a split base station, less time-critical than the DU. Connects to DU over F1.
Call/session log records carrying Non-SA (NSA) tags where NR runs as a secondary node on an LTE anchor and EPC core.
SA NR CSL — same log type as NR CSL but in standalone mode where NR is the primary radio with a 5G core.
Call/session log with lower granularity than TrC — contains KPIs such as radio conditions, RSRP, durations, and session info rather than individual signaling messages.
Trace record with per-message signaling detail for a call session, higher granularity than CSL.
Packed Encoding Rules — compact binary encoding of ASN.1 structures for RRC/NAS Layer-3 messages. Decoupling from PHY does not mean skipping PER encoding.
Physical layer — OFDM, modulation, and channel coding into IQ samples. A real DU must eventually demodulate IQ samples; PHY cannot be skipped entirely.
Orthogonal Frequency Division Multiplexing — splits bandwidth into parallel narrow sub-carriers, each carrying a slow stream. Resource mapping places IQ points on the OFDM time-frequency grid.
Turning bits into a physical radio wave by varying amplitude and phase of a sinusoidal carrier.

Memoryless model where the next procedure depends only on the current state. Built from TrC data as states and transition probabilities between multi-message procedures (RRC setup, registration, etc.).
Open Air Interface — open-source 4G/5G access network implementation including UE, gNB, and other network elements.
Medium Access Control in the DU manages shared radio access: who transmits when, retransmissions, and multiplexing on the time-frequency grid per slot.
Radio Network Temporary Identifier — a 16-bit temporary tag identifying each UE on the radio.
User Equipment — the mobile device (phone/modem) that attaches to the cellular network.
Evolved Node B — the 4G LTE base station connecting UEs to the EPC over S1AP.
Evolved Packet Core — the 4G core network (MME, SGW, PGW, HSS) handling mobility, authentication, and sessions.
Mobility Management Entity — 4G core control node handling attach, authentication, and bearer setup with the eNB over S1AP.
Complex-valued in-phase and quadrature samples representing a modulated radio waveform digitally.
Data Plane Development Kit — high-throughput userspace networking framework often used for U-Plane IQ processing.
Radio Resource Control — Layer 3 protocol between UE and base station for connection setup, mobility, and bearer configuration.
Non-Access Stratum — Layer 3 protocol between UE and core for attach, authentication, and session management.
Physical Resource Block — a unit of frequency-time resources on the LTE/NR grid allocated by the scheduler.
Random Access Channel — procedure for a UE to request initial access to a cell (preamble, response, connection setup).
NG Application Protocol — control-plane protocol between gNB and 5G core AMF.
5G Mobility Management — UE state machine for registration, deregistration, and mobility in 5G SA.
5G Session Management — UE state machine for PDU session establishment, modification, and release.
Stream Control Transmission Protocol — reliable transport used by S1AP, NGAP, and other telecom control protocols.
Femtocell API — software interface between MAC and PHY (or O-DU and PHY) that can bypass full eCPRI fronthaul for lab integration.
Management plane — NETCONF/YANG configuration and capability exchange between O-DU and RU during initialization.
Synchronization plane — PTP/IEEE-1588 timing lock between O-DU and RU; prerequisite before C-plane and U-plane traffic.
O-RAN WG4 fronthaul specification (eCPRI-based) between DU and RU for C-plane and U-plane sections.
Access and Mobility Management Function — 5G core node handling registration and mobility over NGAP.
5G user data session between UE and data network, established via 5GSM procedures.
Network Configuration Protocol — management interface (often with YANG models) for RU/O-DU initialization.
Precision Time Protocol — sub-microsecond synchronization required for O-RAN S-plane between DU and RU.
Hybrid Automatic Repeat Request — L1/L2 retransmission mechanism whose timing RRC procedures depend on.
